pr.title: refine splits and match and link itmaplib.c
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: PR #2549 split the remaining item translation-unit content out of it_2725, introduced and linked melee/it/it_3F14.c, and moved itmaplib.c from NonMatching to Matching. The diff updates splits, symbols, headers, includes, and item-related source files; the bot report shows code/data progress plus temporary-looking broken-match report entries caused by ownership movement between units.

key_files.role: new split-out item translation unit with data/bss/sbss content moved from it_2725 and item logic table/function content
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: splits.txt adds melee/it/it_3F14.c data/bss/sbss ranges
diff_excerpt: it_2725.c removes common item tables and related code while it_3F14.c is added

key_files.role: new header for the split-out it_3F14 unit
source unit reduced after moving content and ownership into it_3F14
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: large block of item includes and common item tables removed
human_pr_text: 'Got the last of the TUs split out of it_2725 in #2546 :)'

key_files.role: target item map library source refined and matched/linked
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/it/itmaplib.c added 190 deleted 117 across 20 hunks
diff_excerpt: configure.py changes itmaplib.c from NonMatching to Matching
bot_report: new matches for itmaplib text symbols it_802759DC, it_802765BC, it_8027737C, it_80276934, it_80277040 and data/sdata2

key_files.role: adjusted text/sdata2 boundary adjacent to itmaplib after split refinement
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: splits.txt changes ithitbox text end from 0x80275BC8 to 0x802759DC and sdata2 end from 0x804DC73C to 0x804DC738

key_files.role: translation-unit section ownership updated for it_3F14, it_2725, ithitbox, and itmaplib
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: new it_3F14 ranges and adjusted it_2725/ithitbox/itmaplib boundaries

key_files.role: symbol ownership/scope/size metadata updated for moved data and literals
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: local @ labels replace address-style symbols for some data/sdata/sdata2 entries; it_804D6D1C size changes from 0x1 to 0x4

key_files.role: build configuration marks it_3F14.c and itmaplib.c as Matching
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: adds Object(Matching, "melee/it/it_3F14.c") and changes itmaplib.c from NonMatching to Matching

key_files.path: multiple source files under src/melee/db, src/melee/ft, src/melee/gr, src/melee/it, and src/melee/it/items
key_files.role: include surface updated to use the new it_3F14 header where needed
evidence_refs: changed_files: many one-line additions across item, fighter collision, ground, and item-specific source files
diff_excerpt: dbitem.c, ftcoll.c, ground.c, it_266F.c, and it_26B1.c add #include "it/it_3F14.h"

what_changed: Added a new split unit, src/melee/it/it_3F14.c, and corresponding header src/melee/it/it_3F14.h.
Moved data/bss/sbss ownership for the 0x803F1418-0x803F1F00 .data range, 0x804A0E30-0x804A0FD8 .bss range, and 0x804D6D00-0x804D6D48 .sbss range from it_2725 into it_3F14 according to splits.txt.
Reduced it_2725.c substantially by removing many item includes and a large common-item table block, consistent with the PR body claim that the last TUs were split out of it_2725.
Adjusted adjacent section boundaries: ithitbox text now ends at 0x802759DC and itmaplib text now starts at 0x802759DC; ithitbox and itmaplib sdata2 boundary moved from 0x804DC73C to 0x804DC738.
Changed configure.py so melee/it/it_3F14.c is built as Matching and melee/it/itmaplib.c changes from NonMatching to Matching.
Updated symbols.txt for local labels, local string/float/double data symbols, and one sbss size correction.
Added it_3F14.h includes across many dependent item and caller files.
Bot report recorded +3072 matched-code bytes, +8512 linked-code bytes, +252 matched-data bytes, and +164 linked-data bytes, with 13 new matches.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired a TU split with matching configuration updates: it_3F14 received explicit split ranges while configure.py added it_3F14.c as Matching and changed itmaplib.c to Matching.
The split kept section ownership explicit instead of compensating in C: splits.txt and symbols.txt were adjusted alongside source movement.
The include surface was updated broadly after adding it_3F14.h, avoiding hidden source-local declarations in dependent files.
evidence_refs: changed_files: many one-line source include changes
diff_excerpt: dbitem.c, ftcoll.c, ground.c, it_266F.c, it_26B1.c include it_3F14.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a translation unit, update splits, symbols, headers, configure.py, and all dependent includes together so ownership movement and build linkage are reviewable as one coherent change.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: last TUs split out of it_2725
diff_excerpt: splits.txt adds it_3F14 and adjusts adjacent units
diff_excerpt: configure.py changes it_3F14.c and itmaplib.c Matching status

decomp_lessons.lesson: Section boundary changes can make bot reports show broken matches in old owners while new owners gain matching data/code; interpret such reports in light of split ownership movement before treating them as source regressions.
evidence_refs: bot_report: broken matches for ithitbox it_802759DC and it_2725 .bss
diff_excerpt: section ranges moved between it_2725, ithitbox, itmaplib, and it_3F14

decomp_lessons.lesson: For data-heavy split refinements, prefer symbols.txt metadata and local-label updates over inventing source-level globals solely to force section order.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: address-style data symbols changed to local @ labels and local scope entries

naming_conventions.convention: New split unit follows address-range style naming, it_3F14.c/it_3F14.h, matching the address-like naming used for not-yet-semantic item units.
Some data/sdata/sdata2 symbols were converted from global address-style names like it_804DC708 to local @ labels where symbols.txt records local scope.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt replaces many it_804DC7xx entries with @### local labels

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Move exact split ranges in splits.txt to transfer ownership of data/bss/sbss/text chunks between adjacent item TUs.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ithitbox and itmaplib text/sdata2 boundaries adjusted

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Promote a source file to Matching in configure.py only after the diff also supplies split/symbol/source changes that the bot report recognizes as 100% matches.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py changes itmaplib.c to Matching and adds it_3F14.c Matching
bot_report: new matches for itmaplib functions and it_3F14 data/bss/sbss

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use symbol metadata changes, including local labels and corrected sizes, to align data ownership during TU splitting.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt local @ labels and it_804D6D1C size correction

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code 68.96% (+0.08%, +3072 bytes), linked code 41.78% (+0.22%, +8512 bytes), matched data 41.20% (+0.02%, +252 bytes), linked data 34.94% (+0.01%, +164 bytes), with 13 new matches.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: The bot also listed 2 broken matches and 1 unmatched-item regression: ithitbox it_802759DC, it_2725 .bss, and it_2725 .sbss. The PR evidence shows these coincide with split-boundary and ownership movement, but no human review comment explains intent.

follow_up_queries: symbols.txt @ labels local data split ownership it_3F14

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merged state, counts, changed files
raw_diff/diff_excerpt: representative split, symbol, configure, include, and it_2725 changes
notes: The raw diff and raw PR JSON loaded files are truncated, so detailed claims about every hunks' exact source logic are avoided.
There are no human review comments in the slice; review_feedback is limited to the bot report and does not infer reviewer intent.
The bot report provides progress numbers and broken-match/regression entries, but the intake does not independently verify builds or objdiff.
The PR title/body support the broad split/match/link purpose, but not detailed rationale for each symbol rename or every include addition.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Bundle TU split ownership updates across splits, symbols, headers, configure, and includes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2549 demonstrates a TU split workflow: it_3F14.c was split out of it_2725, splits.txt and symbols.txt were updated for ownership, configure.py marked the new/target units Matching, and dependent files included the new header.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret bot broken matches during split moves as ownership signals, not automatically source regressions
accepted_candidate_records.text: The bot reported new matches in it_3F14 and itmaplib while also listing broken matches in prior/adjacent owners. In this PR, the diff shows section boundaries moving between it_2725, ithitbox, itmaplib, and it_3F14, so curator review should treat these report entries as split-ownership context unless separate evidence shows a real regression.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/it/it_3F14.c owns item data/bss/sbss ranges split from it_2725 in PR #2549
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2549 adds melee/it/it_3F14.c to splits.txt with .data 0x803F1418-0x803F1F00, .bss 0x804A0E30-0x804A0FD8, and .sbss 0x804D6D00-0x804D6D48, while narrowing it_2725.c data ownership to 0x803F1F00-0x803F1F38.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential durable path fact about TU section ownership introduced by this merged PR; should be curated against current splits before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: itmaplib.c became a Matching object in PR #2549
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2549 changes configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/itmaplib.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/itmaplib.c") and adds Object(Matching, "melee/it/it_3F14.c").
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate path/configuration fact; verify against current configure.py before adding to source corpus.

source_update_candidates.title: TU split report interpretation
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR moves section ownership between translation units, bot reports may show broken matches in the old owner and new matches in the new owner. Review split/symbol ownership changes before classifying those entries as regressions.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a possible general review standard derived from the PR, but it should remain curator-reviewed because there is no human comment explicitly stating this policy.

rejection_notes: Do not claim all itmaplib functions are matched from this slice; the bot report names only several specific new function matches and aggregate progress.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no review comments are present.
Do not promote every symbol.txt local-label change as a naming convention without checking current source and symbol ownership.
Do not claim the bot-reported broken matches were intentionally ignored by reviewers; the evidence only shows merge metadata and related split movement.
